title: Virtual-thread use cases deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Virtual-thread use cases; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Virtual-thread use cases: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Virtual threads make CPU-bound code run in parallel without limits and remove the need for timeouts, cancellation, or resource bounds.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use virtual threads for numerous blocking tasks, but still bound scarce resources and use CPU-appropriate parallelism for compute-bound work.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Use virtual threads for numerous blocking tasks, but still bound scarce resources and use CPU-appropriate parallelism for compute-bound work.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Use virtual threads for numerous blocking tasks, but still bound scarce resources and use CPU-appropriate parallelism for compute-bound work.
Virtual threads make CPU-bound code run in parallel without limits and remove the need for timeouts, cancellation, or resource bounds.

answer.explanation: Use virtual threads for numerous blocking tasks, but still bound scarce resources and use CPU-appropriate parallelism for compute-bound work.

items.title: Virtual-thread use cases: canonical checkpoint

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint phrase from the operational correction, then read the complete sentence for semantic fit.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'blocking tasks'. Use virtual threads for numerous blocking tasks, but still bound scarce resources and use CPU-appropriate parallelism for compute-bound work.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Use virtual threads for numerous blocking tasks, but still bound scarce resources and use CPU-appropriate parallelism for compute-bound work.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.
